title: Indian payments chief thinks AI will be heavily involved in next era of digital payment growth
gist: NPCI CEO Dilip Asbe sees AI as the driver for UPI's next growth phase — from reaching new users via voice and multilingual tools to fraud detection and credit access for merchants with digital footprints.

title: Instagram is testing more ways to customize ‘Your Algorithm’
gist: Instagram is testing new ways to surface its 'Your Algorithm' content-tuning feature more prominently, including pull-down menus in the feed and swipe-up prompts on Reels, with the stated goal of making it central to the user experience.

title: The best response to AI slop and online noise is from Robin Williams
gist: The piece uses Robin Williams's Good Will Hunting bench speech as a framework for arguing that lived human experience — not aggregated knowledge — is what gives creative and professional work meaning in an era of AI-generated content.

title: India Ecommerce Paths for Foreign Sellers
gist: India's e-commerce market is $125B but heavily regulated for foreign sellers, who must choose between owning a marketplace, partnering with local distributors, selling D2C with significant legal hurdles, or shipping cross-border with high import duties and slow delivery.

title: The loyalty metric no one wants to explain
gist: Most loyalty and rewards programs report repeat purchase rates without measuring incrementality — i.e., they cannot prove whether customers returned because of the incentive or would have returned anyway, making many reported 'loyalty' outcomes statistically meaningless.

title: Stocky is shutting down, but that’s not the real story
gist: Shopify is shutting down its Stocky inventory app in August, with the article (sponsored by Katana) arguing this reflects that most growing brands already sell across multiple channels and need multi-channel inventory management, not a single-store tool.

title: How 3 brands are using TikTok Shop to expand abroad
gist: Three brands — a gaming console maker, a K-beauty brand, and a luxury resale platform — describe using U.S. TikTok Shop momentum as a launchpad for international expansion into the U.K., Europe, and Japan, emphasizing localized creator partnerships and platform-native experimentation.

title: Streaming is the next frontier for Walmart’s, Kroger’s ads businesses
gist: Walmart agreed to acquire connected TV ad platform Vibe.co for over $1B, continuing its push to let advertisers use Walmart shopper data to prove that streaming TV ads drive in-store and online purchases.

title: Amazon’s Prime Day drives online sales in the US up 9.3%
gist: U.S. online retail spend grew 9.3% YoY to $26.4 billion during Amazon's Prime Day (June 23–26), with shoppers trading up to more expensive items and BNPL usage jumping 9.5%. The event is now approaching Black Friday-scale spend, with roughly 40% of tracked retailers running deeper promotions than last year.

title: What’s next for international shipping as EU ends de minimis exemption
gist: The EU is eliminating its €150 de minimis exemption on July 1, replacing it with a temporary flat €3 customs duty per declared line item on low-value packages. A five-SKU shipment under €150 will now incur a €15 fee rather than no duty at all.

title: Consumer spending rises as inflation speeds up to three-year high
gist: U.S. consumer spending rose 0.7% in May despite PCE inflation hitting 4.1% annually, the fastest pace in three years, while income rose at the same rate. Markets now price an 80% chance the Fed raises rates by year-end, with core inflation expected to stay above target through 2028.

title: Electronics prices are starting to soar, adding to the stress consumers are facing
gist: Apple, Microsoft, and Nintendo have all announced significant price increases on laptops, tablets, and game consoles, driven by a memory and storage chip shortage caused by AI data center demand. The MacBook Pro gained $300, Xbox consoles will rise $150–$200 in August, and Nintendo Switch 2 will increase $50 in September.

title: Amazon launches AI assistant for German sellers
gist: Amazon has launched its AI-powered Seller Assistant inside Seller Central in Germany, designed to help sellers find information and make business decisions more efficiently.

title: Search Console’s New AI Data Is a Start
gist: Google Search Console has added a 'Generative AI' section showing URL impressions in AI Overviews and AI Mode, but the report has no click-through data, no feature-specific filters, and counts impressions even when users never see the cited URL. Site owners can now also block their content from appearing in Google's AI answers.

title: Bogg, on track for record sales, expands into Urban Outfitters and Anthropologie
gist: Bogg, a TikTok-viral washable tote brand founded in 2008, is expanding into six new retailers including Urban Outfitters and Anthropologie, bringing its total U.S. retail footprint to over 7,000 locations. The brand attributes its recent growth surge to social media, influencer endorsements, and a collector-driven fanbase.

title: Digiday+ Research: The marketers’ 2026 guide to creator marketing, including Duolingo, Ulta and YouTube
gist: U.S. brands are projected to spend $13.7 billion on influencer marketing by 2027, with creator marketing expanding beyond social feeds into broader channel strategies. Digiday+ Research analyzes how brands like Duolingo and Ulta are building creator marketing into their overall mix.

title: Nike says it expects $986 million in IEEPA tariff refunds
gist: Nike expects to recover approximately $986 million in IEEPA tariff refunds from the U.S. government, which drove an 890 basis point improvement in gross margin for its fiscal Q4 2026. The bulk of the refunds — $965 million — are tied to its North America business, with an additional $21 million from Converse.

title: Combatting Returns Fraud, Without Creating Friction
gist: Returns fraud is estimated to cost UK retailers over £5 billion annually, with analysis of one million real-world returns identifying £29 million in potentially fraudulent activity. ReBound's new Radar system uses physical verification, photographic evidence, and AI-driven data analysis to flag fraud without creating friction for legitimate shoppers.

title: How Dsquared2 and Damiani turned checkout friction into a growth lever
gist: Brands like Dsquared2 and Damiani are finding that international e-commerce growth stalls not at discovery but at operational execution — pricing, duties, payments, and delivery friction are the real barriers. ESW research across 23,000 consumers in 18 markets found that nearly half of U.S. shoppers cite shipping costs as a barrier to cross-border purchases.

title: Primark nears 50 US stores with latest openings
gist: Primark is opening two new U.S. stores in Houston and Indianapolis, bringing its total U.S. count to 44 locations as it pushes toward 50 stores with a value-pricing strategy. The expansion comes alongside recent leadership changes and a planned separation from parent company Associated British Foods by end of 2027.

title: Adult women are driving toy industry growth
gist: Adult women over 18 accounted for more than half of U.S. toy industry growth from January through April 2026, with overall adult consumers driving 35% of total category growth. Social commerce — particularly TikTok Shop — is a significant discovery engine, with toys, hobbies, and collectibles ranking as the third-largest category on the platform.

title: Consumer sentiment rises from record low on prospect of lower inflation
gist: U.S. consumer sentiment rose 10% in June from a record low, driven by falling gas prices following a U.S.-Iran ceasefire framework, though year-ahead inflation expectations remain elevated at 4.6%. Personal consumption expenditures rose 4.1% annually in May, and consumers continue to report that high overall prices are weighing on their finances.

title: Rhode Island becomes first state to mandate staffing ratio for self-checkout
gist: Rhode Island has become the first U.S. state to mandate a staffing ratio for self-checkout, requiring at least one worker per three self-checkout stations in grocery stores, effective January 1, 2027. Several other states including California, New York, and Massachusetts are considering similar restrictions aimed at reducing theft and improving customer experience.

title: Brands Briefing: Measurement is the next frontier in GEO for brands
gist: Loftie, a wellness startup, tracked that the share of customers discovering it via AI prompt engines like ChatGPT and Gemini doubled from 1.5% to 3% in six months, surfaced through post-purchase surveys. The trend points to GEO (generative engine optimization) measurement becoming a serious priority for DTC brands.

title: Cost-per-click increased by 15% in 1 year
gist: Google Ads cost-per-click rose 15% (about €0.06) between June 2024 and June 2025 across all campaign types, while return on ad spend dropped by more than 40% over the same period.

title: Prime Day results suggest shoppers are saving splurges for Black Friday
gist: Analysis of this year's Prime Day results suggests consumers may be holding back discretionary spending for Black Friday rather than buying during the July event.

title: How Supergoop CMO Lauren Weinberg is tackling Amazon and TikTok Shop amid channel mix expansion
gist: Supergoop CMO Lauren Weinberg discusses how the sunscreen brand is navigating channel expansion across Amazon and TikTok Shop while maintaining its brand positioning.

title: Kroger is back in growth mode with Giant Eagle acquisition
gist: Kroger is acquiring regional grocer Giant Eagle for just under $1.7 billion, its first major growth move after its blocked Albertsons merger, as it faces intensifying competition from Walmart, Amazon, and discount grocers.

title: Dick’s adds paid loyalty tier for $99 a year
gist: Dick's Sporting Goods is launching ScoreCard+, a $99 annual paid loyalty tier offering free shipping, $100 in quarterly rewards, and exclusive discounts, targeting its existing 30 million loyalty members who already drive over 75% of sales.

title: Fashion Briefing: ‘What the people want’: Bootleg sports merchandise is pushing licensed brands to step up their game
gist: Bootleg sports merchandise featuring unlicensed creative designs surged during the NBA Finals and World Cup, signaling consumer demand for culturally resonant product that official licensed brands are failing to supply.

title: New Ecommerce Tools: July 1, 2026
gist: A weekly roundup of new e-commerce tools covering integrations and launches across reviews, AI-generated content, fraud prevention, livestreaming, agentic commerce, and autonomous delivery.

title: Are Nike’s ‘kernels of progress’ enough?
gist: Nike reported Q4 revenues down 1% to $11 billion, with continued double-digit declines in China and Converse, though gross margin improved significantly partly due to a $986 million tariff refund; analysts are divided on whether the turnaround is gaining real traction.

title: Back-to-school season has become a summer-long event, thanks to an earlier Prime Day
gist: Amazon's decision to move Prime Day to late June has effectively made back-to-school shopping a summer-long event, with Target and Walmart following suit and smaller retailers adjusting their strategies to capture deal-seeking parents earlier in the season.

title: Ulta Beauty Strategies: What Ulta is learning about Gen Alpha consumers
gist: Ulta Beauty is building a dedicated strategy to acquire Gen Alpha (born 2010–2024) consumers by first investing in understanding their motivations and then designing tailored in-store and brand experiences around them.

title: Target grows marketplace with Forever 21, Clarks brand additions
gist: Target has expanded its invite-only Target Plus marketplace with brands including Forever 21, Clarks, and JanSport, and is using AI agents to screen seller applicants and accelerate category growth like K-beauty.

title: Marketplace Briefing: How a cartoon frog wizard is helping Clorox sell Pine-Sol on TikTok Shop
gist: Clorox launched four limited-edition Pine-Sol scents exclusively on TikTok Shop, tied to fictional cartoon characters already embedded in the brand's online identity, and sold out within hours.

title: Amazon tightens Fulfilled by Merchant requirements
gist: Amazon is enforcing stricter on-time delivery requirements for Fulfilled by Merchant sellers, requiring a 90% or higher on-time delivery rate in Germany and rolling out similar policies in other markets.

title: Reformation’s proposed IPO: 4 notable numbers
gist: Reformation's IPO filing shows 75% of new DTC customers acquired through unpaid channels, a 9% marketing-to-revenue ratio, and omnichannel shoppers spending 3.1x more annually than single-channel customers.

title: Stitch Fix expands AI image generation to improve personalization
gist: Stitch Fix has expanded its AI-powered Vision feature to let users generate on-demand images of themselves wearing recommended outfits, building a personalized style library within the app.

title: Marketing has a measurement problem that most brands are ignoring
gist: A commentary from the CEO of measurement firm Measured argues that platform-reported attribution is structurally flawed, with each channel claiming full credit for sales and brands unknowingly spending budget re-acquiring customers who would have converted anyway.

title: Costco is the anti-Amazon
gist: A long-form analysis argues that Costco's model — low SKU count, pallet-level logistics, in-store shopping, and constrained assortment — is more cost-efficient and socially sustainable than Amazon's infinite-assortment, doorstep-delivery approach, with Costco's SG&A at ~10% of sales versus Amazon's ~40% delivery cost on non-AWS revenue. Despite running counter to every 'future of retail' assumption, Costco has grown revenue over 10% annually for five years.

title: Avoid All-Caps Headlines, Says CRO Pro
gist: CRO practitioner Nate Lagos describes a 'bottoms-up' testing approach — starting with copy closest to the add-to-cart button — and shares a surprising A/B test result where switching landing page headlines from all-caps to title case produced a 25% conversion lift on a high-traffic page.

title: Glossy Pop Newsletter: How Meta leveraged Kylie Jenner, Peggy Gou and Substackers in its first fashion campaign
gist: Meta launched a fashion campaign for its Meta Glasses, positioning the product as an accessories line and working with figures like Kylie Jenner and Peggy Gou to reach fashion consumers and women broadly.

title: GPT-5.5 Codex reasoning-token clustering may be leading to degraded performance
gist: A developer found that GPT-5.5 responses in Codex disproportionately cluster at exactly 516 reasoning tokens, with the pattern sharply worsening in May–June 2026 and correlating with lower overall reasoning-token usage, suggesting a hidden budget cap or throttling behavior specific to that model.

title: Alibaba reportedly bans employees from using Claude Code
gist: Alibaba is banning employees from using Anthropic's Claude Code starting July 10, citing it as high-risk software, and directing staff to its own Qoder tool instead; Anthropic has been tightening access restrictions for Chinese users.

title: Better Models: Worse Tools
gist: Newer Claude models (Opus 4.8, Sonnet 5) are increasingly generating tool calls with invented, schema-violating fields — a regression not seen in older models — likely because post-training is heavily optimized for Claude Code's own forgiving, flat tool schema rather than general-purpose tool schemas.

title: Commerce media’s measurement problem has an in-store answer
gist: Post-transaction media placed on POS terminals and self-checkout kiosks ties ad exposures directly to shopper IDs and transaction records, enabling deterministic closed-loop attribution that solves retail media's incrementality measurement gap. Brands measuring incrementality report 54% less wasted spend and 49% higher new customer acquisition, yet only 15% have strong measurement confidence today.

title: AI in retail is only as good as its data foundation
gist: AI-driven personalization and agentic commerce in retail only work as well as the underlying customer data quality — dirty, duplicate, or incomplete records undermine recommendations, automation, and hyper-personalization. BCG reports a 4,700% YoY traffic increase to US retail sites from GenAI browsers, signaling that retailers must have clean, API-ready data to stay competitive.

title: Tariffs, trade shocks and the end of retail planning as we know it
gist: Tariff volatility is compounding an existing retail planning crisis where buying decisions made 12–18 months out are colliding with unpredictable costs across shipping, freight, customs, and currency — and most planning infrastructure wasn't built to handle it. Over 75% of supply chain executives expect higher disruption in 2026, and IHL Group estimates $1.73 trillion is lost annually from inventory imbalances caused by slow, disconnected planning.

title: WTF is AI poisoning?
gist: Marketers are increasingly aware that AI search responses have become a competitive arena where brands can potentially influence how rivals are perceived, not just how they themselves appear. Users tend to trust AI-generated results as neutral, making AI poisoning — deliberately skewing LLM outputs against competitors — a real brand risk.

title: Retailers like Target, Aldi take pages from collectibles culture with drops, blind boxes
gist: Major retailers including Target, Trader Joe's, and Aldi are borrowing tactics from collectibles culture — limited drops, blind boxes, and creator collaborations — to drive urgency and excitement across food, apparel, and everyday categories. These mechanics, popularized by toys like Labubu dolls, are now being applied broadly to generate scarcity-driven demand at scale.

title: Hostinger Reframes Site-Free Ecommerce
gist: Hostinger launched a tool that converts a product photo into an AI-generated checkout link with no website required, positioning it as an entry point for selling across social, messaging, and AI channels. The announcement reflects a broader industry shift where ecommerce platforms are decoupling the store from the transaction, as checkout surfaces proliferate across feeds, marketplaces, and AI agents.

title: Dentsu strikes Meta deal to build plumbing for mass influencer activation
gist: Dentsu is integrating Meta's Creator Marketplace and Partnership Ads into its internal operating system via API, allowing clients to manage creator selection, social listening, and paid activation in one dashboard.

title: Why Forme launched a World Cup promotion hedged on Kalshi
gist: Sportswear brand Forme ran a World Cup promotion offering 100% refunds if the US Men's National Team reached the final, hedged through prediction market platform Kalshi — driving a 4X week-over-week traffic increase despite the team's elimination.

title: Rewriting the returns rulebook: Deploying returns personalisation to improve CX and streamline operations
gist: UK e-commerce returns cost retailers an estimated £60 billion annually, and over half of Gen Z shoppers now identify as serial returners — up from 15% three years ago — with traditional deterrents like fees losing effectiveness as personalized, AI-driven returns policies emerge as the next frontier.

title: Ulta’s chief marketer on blending brand building and omnichannel marketing
gist: Ulta Beauty CMO Kelly Mahoney outlines how the brand uses its 47-million-member loyalty program, a proprietary 'Beauty Graph,' retail media network UB Media, TikTok Shop, and in-store experiential events to create a connected omnichannel marketing flywheel.

title: California’s new packaging laws are changing the economics of plastic
gist: California's SB 54 requires brands to pay material-specific fees for packaging waste, with higher charges for non-recyclable single-use plastics, forcing brands that sell in the state to reassess their packaging economics.

title: Dollar Shave Club’s bet: AI makes agencies optional, not obsolete
gist: Dollar Shave Club produces 90% of its advertising in-house and its chief brand officer sees AI as a tool to extend that self-sufficiency further, while still viewing specialist agencies as occasionally useful for a challenger brand.

title: Walmart cuts prices on groceries and other goods
gist: Walmart and Sam's Club are rolling back prices on thousands of summer grocery and seasonal items, citing ongoing consumer pressure from inflation and high gas prices. Food-at-home prices rose 2.7% annually in May, and the initiative covers both in-store and online channels.

title: Staples, Dollar General tout 2025 pricing for back-to-school sales
gist: Staples and Dollar General are launching back-to-school campaigns anchored around $1-and-under pricing, while JLL data shows BTS budgets rose 11.7% YoY to $489 per child, driven by middle-income families. Analysts flag this as a consumer bifurcation signal, with lower-income shoppers facing the most strain.

title: New Ecommerce Tools: July 7, 2026
gist: A weekly roundup of new e-commerce tools covers launches across one-click checkout, agentic commerce, AI-generated product images, logistics, Amazon marketing, and omnichannel marketing automation, among others. Notable items include Square's ChatGPT and Claude integrations, CMA CGM's $1.4B acquisition of FedEx Supply Chain, and Lantern's platform for tracking product visibility inside AI shopping portals.

title: Brands Briefing: Salomon is working to double its US store count by 2028
gist: Salomon is planning to grow its U.S. standalone store count from 6 to 12–15 by end of 2027, targeting cities like Beverly Hills, Boston, San Francisco, and Miami as it approaches its 80th anniversary. The European performance footwear and apparel brand is making a deliberate push to expand its North American physical retail presence.

title: How Bandit Running is expanding internationally while staying hyperlocal
gist: Bandit Running has built a loyal following by combining a streetwear-influenced design aesthetic with deep investment in hyperlocal running communities in New York City, including hosting club runs and sponsoring races. The brand is now expanding internationally while trying to preserve that community-first identity.

title: How category creators like Willow and Oura ward off dupes and defend their leads
gist: Category-creating brands like Willow (wearable breast pump) and Oura (smart ring) face the challenge of defending their market position once they've proven out demand and attracted imitators. The article explores how first-mover DTC innovators build defensible leads against dupes and fast followers.

title: For luxury, the in-store experience still reigns
gist: An EY survey of 1,600+ aspirational luxury consumers (household income $100K+) across 11 markets finds that 71% prefer buying at brand-owned stores, and in-store satisfaction significantly outpaces brand websites. While 94% believe AI can enhance their experience, customers prioritize pampering, human interaction, and personalized style advice as the top in-store value drivers.

title: Galaxus now biggest online store in Switzerland
gist: Galaxus surpassed Zalando to become Switzerland's largest online store, reaching 2.5 billion euros in revenue by end of 2025, as Galaxus accelerated growth while Zalando stagnated.

title: D+ Research: Marketers hesitate to adopt AI for influencer and CTV marketing
gist: A Digiday+ Research Q1 2026 survey of 100+ marketers found only 25% use AI for influencer marketing, even as AI adoption for social and retail media marketing grows faster.

title: OpenAI set to expand ads to France, Germany and Ireland
gist: OpenAI is expanding its advertising pilot from the UK into France, Germany, and Ireland, hiring regional ad sales leaders across EMEA to build out its commercial ads business in Europe.

title: Fashion Briefing: As Gen Z goes back to the mall, Garage is opening 20 profitable stores a year
gist: Canadian Gen Z fashion brand Garage is opening 20 new stores per year, achieving 30% profit margins and breaking even on each new location within 18 months on average, while JCPenney's CEO notes Gen Z is returning to physical malls.

title: On is betting on robots and nearshoring to gain an advantage
gist: Athletic brand On is expanding its robotic LightSpray manufacturing system from Switzerland and South Korea toward Europe and the US, aiming to produce smaller batches closer to demand and reduce markdown-driven inventory risk.

title: Retailers plan to invest deeper in AI, cybersecurity this year
gist: A KPMG survey of 250 retail executives found 52% invest $50M+ annually in digital tech, with 74% expecting to deploy AI at scale within 12 months, and cybersecurity (52%), data/analytics (49%), and AI/automation (42%) topping planned investment areas.

title: The running list of retail deals in 2026
gist: Retail Dive's 2026 deal tracker notes M&A activity remains selective amid macro uncertainty, IPOs are recovering but retail accounts for just 1.5% of Q1 2026 IPOs, and megadeals are driving most deal value across consumer markets.

title: Marketplace Briefing: AI traffic is poised to reshape the Amazon funnel
gist: Amazon agency Envision Horizons launched a beta AI visibility tracker to measure how referral traffic from ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini influences purchases on Amazon, reflecting growing agency interest in understanding generative AI's role in the shopping funnel.

title: Meta introduces AI-powered room visualization feature
gist: Meta AI now lets shoppers upload photos of their rooms and visualize catalog products placed inside them, pulling from existing ad catalog data, with the ability to compare options and purchase directly through the brand's website.

title: Back-to-school shoppers head to mass merchants, department stores
gist: Deloitte's 2026 back-to-school survey projects flat spending at $557 per child (down 6% inflation-adjusted), with mass merchants dominating and 57% of parents expecting the economy to worsen — but parents using generative AI tools plan to spend the most, averaging $737 per child.

title: To manage 300,000 creators, Unilever automates everything but the relationship
gist: Unilever scaled its creator program from 10,000 to 300,000 influencers and is now automating discovery, vetting, and content approvals while keeping human judgment at the center of actual relationship management.

title: Phia accused of ‘cookie stuffing,’ taking affiliate credit on purchases it didn’t earn
gist: Phia, a shopping browser extension that finds lowest prices and discount codes, has been accused of 'cookie stuffing' — secretly opening background tabs to override affiliate referral codes from other sources and claim commissions on purchases it didn't drive. The company was suspended from affiliate platform Impact.com; it says the issue has been fixed.

title: Brands won this season of ‘Love Island USA’
gist: Love Island USA's season 8 drew 1.31 billion viewing minutes in its first week (up 69% YoY), and ad sales across the show and its spinoff rose 73% versus last season, with over 1,000 media partners and seven integrated brand sponsors. Brands are increasingly integrating into the show's content rather than running traditional ads.

title: New York City to ban deceptive subscription practices
gist: New York City is banning deceptive subscription practices starting October 1, requiring easy cancellation and imposing fines of $525 per affected subscription; it is also proposing rules that mandate total upfront price disclosure, including all mandatory fees. The subscription rule could save NYC residents an estimated $162.5 million per year.

title: How Aéropostale entertains Gen Alpha with a creator-led mini-series
gist: Aéropostale launched a creator-led serialized mini-series ('Intern Diaries') starring TikTok creator Déjà Clark, pulling 3.5 million views on its first two episodes; a tied exclusive app-first collection drop strategy drove 30% YoY customer growth and previously catapulted the brand's app into the App Store top 5. The brand is also linking loyalty programs with J.C. Penney to drive cross-shopping.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
gist: The European Commission has preliminarily found that Meta's features — autoplay, infinite scroll, and highly personalized recommendations — violate the Digital Services Act by creating addictive design without adequate risk assessment, and is ordering changes or fines up to 6% of global turnover.

title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
gist: The EU found Meta in breach of the Digital Services Act over addictive design features including infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and personalized recommendation algorithms, and is demanding Meta disable these features by default and reduce engagement-maximizing algorithmic behavior or face fines of up to 6% of global turnover.

title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
gist: Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging that former Apple employees, including OpenAI's Chief Hardware Officer Tang Tan, systematically stole trade secrets — including hardware specs, prototypes, and proprietary manufacturing techniques — to benefit OpenAI's nascent hardware business.

title: Apple sues OpenAI, accuses ex-employees of stealing trade secrets
gist: Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI details allegations that former Apple executives and engineers at OpenAI used job interviews to extract confidential hardware information, coached departing employees to evade Apple's security protocols, and used Apple's proprietary manufacturing techniques without authorization — with over 400 former Apple employees now at OpenAI.
